title: Jacobaea arnautorum
taxonomy: Jacobaea arnautorum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the sunflower family, Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Jacobaea, which consists of approximately 20 species of plants. Jacobaea arnautorum is native to the Balkan Peninsula and is also found in some parts of Europe. The classification of Jacobaea arnautorum is further supported by its morphological characteristics, including its leaf shape and flower structure. Within the genus Jacobaea, Jacobaea arnautorum is often placed in the section Jacobaea, sect. Jacobaea.
uses: Jacobaea arnautorum is known to be used in traditional medicine, although limited information is available. It is reported to have been used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and flowers are said to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Research suggests that Jacobaea arnautorum may also possess antimicrobial and antioxidant properties.
